short_term.title: Short-term view (0–30 days · next earnings cycle)
bullets: Run-rate $21.15B in FY2026 Q1, +10.3% YoY — accelerating trajectory entering the next print.
Next-quarter consensus: $22.30B (+5.4% vs current Q) — set by 26 broker contributors.
EPS consensus next qtr: $4.45 vs current $4.41.
Margin context: 5.0% net margin (6.4% op margin) — compressed — watch operating leverage.
Next earnings: 2026-06-23 — primary catalyst for the short-term window.
short_term.takeaway: Going into the next earnings print, the setup is mixed — execution risk on the next guide is elevated.

long_term.title: Long-term view (90+ days · multi-year setup)
bullets: TTM revenue $80B — in cyclical drawdown.
Sell-side: FY1 $88B → FY2 $92B (+4.0% multi-year top-line implied).
Forward EBIT margin: 15.0% — average operating profile over the long-cycle view.
6-quarter run-rate CAGR: +12.3% — the secular trajectory the market is pricing.
Sector thesis (Industrials): Reshoring + defense supercycle drives a multi-year backlog conversion story.
long_term.takeaway: Long-cycle thesis is intact if the next 2–4 quarters confirm a stabilization in trajectory.

short_term.title: Short-term view (0–45 DTE · front-month tactical)
bullets: Front-month ATM IV: 58.3% — elevated (premium-selling favored).
Put/Call OI: 1.36 — put-heavy hedging (institutions defensive).
30-day implied move: ±7.6% ($343.45 – $399.93) — the 1-σ range the options market is pricing.
ATM skew: -28.76pp — call skew (upside bid — bullish positioning).

long_term.title: Long-term view (90+ DTE · LEAPS / structural)
bullets: Back-month avg IV (90+ DTE): 35.1% across 7 expiries.
Available long-dated expiries: 7 dates spanning 126–616 DTE.
398-day implied move: ±37.7% ($231.71 – $511.67) — the long-dated 1-σ range.
Sell-side PT vs options-implied range: consensus $265 (-28.7% upside). Compare to the 90+ DTE implied range above.
Term structure in backwardation (-23.2pp front→back) — near-term event premium; long-dated vol cheaper.
long_term.strategy: Sell 6-month strangle: short $335 put / short $409 call. Range-bound + high IV → premium-selling on both sides; profits if spot stays within ±10%; manage with rolls or assignment.
